sentences: Dayavan: Truth is Power (English: "The Compassionate") is a 1988 Hindi thriller movie remake of the Tamil film "Nayagan" (1987).
The movie was directed by Feroz Khan, and starred Vinod Khanna, Feroz Khan and Madhuri Dixit in lead roles with Aditya Pancholi in an important role.
Girish Kohli (born November 29, 1983 in Mumbai) is an Indian author and screenwriter.
He is the writer of the Hindi thriller movie MOM.
Dharmatama is a 1975 Hindi thriller movie and the first Bollywood film to be shot in Afghanistan.
It was produced and directed by Feroz Khan.
The movie is the first attempt in India to localise "The Godfather".
The title character was based on matka (form of Indian gambling) king Ratan Khatri.
It is said that Khan sat down with Khatri to learn more about him and understand the nuances of matka.
The cast includes Feroz Khan, Hema Malini, Rekha, Premnath, Imtiaz Khan, Danny Denzongpa, Farida Jalal, Ranjeet, Helen, Madan Puri, Jeevan, Iftekhar and Dara Singh, satyen kappu and sudhir.
The music is by Kalyanji Anandji.
This film took Feroz Khan to new heights in his career as this was a blockbuster movie.
The same year, "Sholay" and "Deewar" were released.
The film is also its scenes featuring Buzkashi a Central Asian sport on horses, including aerial shots, which in turn won film's cinematographer, Kamal Bose, Filmfare Award for Best Cinematographer.

Kites is a 2010 Indian Hindi/Spanish mix language dialogue romantic action thriller film directed by Anurag Basu, story written and produced by Rakesh Roshan, starring Hrithik Roshan, Bárbara Mori, Kangana Ranaut, and Kabir Bedi.
The film was released in India and in North America on 21 May 2010.
Its 208-theater opening in North America made it the largest Bollywood release up to that time.
It was also the first Bollywood movie to reach the weekend top ten, though "My Name is Khan" had a larger first-weekend North American gross.
Reliance Entertainment bought worldwide distribution rights of Kites for whooping () , which is a record sum .
Despite a strong opening, The film only managed to collect () net in its lifetime run following a critical loss.
The film had been aired in a week of its release on smallscreen .

A betting pool, sports lottery, sweep, or office pool if done at work, is a form of gambling, specifically a variant of parimutuel betting influenced by lotteries, where gamblers pay a fixed price into a "pool" (from which taxes and a house "take" or "vig" are removed), and then make a selection on an outcome, usually related to sport.
In an informal game, the vig is usually quite small or non-existent.
The pool is evenly divided between those that have made the correct selection.
There are no odds involved; each winner's payoff depends simply on the number of gamblers and the number of winners.
(True parimutuel betting, which was historically referred to as pool betting, involves both odds calculations and variable wager amounts.)
Khoon Bhari Maang (English: Blood on the Head) is an Indian Hindi thriller movie released on 12 August 1988, directed and produced by Rakesh Roshan.
It is a remake of the Australian mini-series "Return to Eden" (1983), and is about a wealthy widow who is almost killed by her lover and sets out for revenge.
The film was a comeback venture for Rekha and was an critical and commercial success.
The film was remade in Telugu as "Gowthami" starring Suhasini.
Lion of Gujarat is a 2015 Bollywood action drama directed by Dinesh Lamba and produced by Jitendra Gohil.

This is a first Bollywood film shot entirely in Gujarat starring Dinesh Lamba, Homi Wadia and Akruti Agrawal in lead roles and Milan Vaidya, Manoj Parmar, Arman Tahil, Uday Danger, Yashpal, Gazal Rai, Bharat Thakkar in supporting role.
The move was shot in the locations of Jamnagar, Porbandar and other parts of Gujarat.
"Lion of Gujarat" is scheduled for release on 26 June 2015.
"Aama" (आमा) (Literally: Mother) is the first Nepali movie produced in Nepal, starring Shiva Shankar Manandhar and Bhuvan Chand (Thapa) as the leading actors.
The movie was produced by the Information Department of the Nepalese Government and released on October 7, 1964.
Bollywood film maker Hira Singh Khatri was invited by the late King Mahendra to direct the first Nepali movie.
Baaz: A Bird in Danger is 2003 Hindi thriller movie directed by Tinnu Verma and starring Sunil Shetty, Karisma Kapoor, Jackie Shroff.
Other cast members include Dino Morea, Preeti Jhangiani Aditi Gowitrikar and Suhasini Mulay.
Matka" gambling or satta" is a form of lottery which originally involved betting on the opening and closing rates of cotton transmitted from the New York Cotton Exchange.
It originates from before the era of Indian independence when it was known as "Ankada Jugar" ("figures gambling").
In the 1960s, the system was replaced with other ways of generating random numbers, including pulling slips from a large earthenware pot known as a "matka", or dealing playing cards.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Produce a single, concise, structured first-hop evidence report as the `summary`. The summary must do all of the following and only include content that helps determine what to fetch next:

- Enumerate concrete facts found in the provided passages. For each fact include the passage source label and either a very short quoted fragment (up to about 15 words) when a decisive short fact appears or a brief paraphrase otherwise. Keep each fact to one short line.

- Explicitly identify the bridge entity(ies) and any missing or ambiguous fact(s) that prevent answering the question from the given passages. If a name or identifier in the question corresponds to multiple candidate pages present in `passages`, list every candidate variant and mark the name as ambiguous; do not choose between them.

- Prescribe concrete, prioritized next-hop retrieval targets to resolve each missing or ambiguous element. For each target, specify which page(s) or disambiguation page to fetch and what exact section, keyword, or datum to extract (for example: specific field such as birth year, office held, list membership, dates, or the section heading to check). Prioritize targets (1, 2, 3...) when helpful.

- Only produce a final answer in `summary` if every required fact for every entity is present and unambiguous in the supplied passages; otherwise do not produce a final answer and focus on retrieval guidance.

Formatting and content constraints:
- Keep the summary concise (a short paragraph or a few short bullet-like lines).
- Produce only the single output field `summary` and nothing else.
- Preserve source-linked evidence; do not assert facts without a passage label.
- Do not invent facts or assume which ambiguous candidate is intended.
- Avoid copying long verbatim spans; use short quoted fragments when decisive and paraphrase otherwise.
- When an expected authoritative page is missing from `passages`, note that absence and include that page title as a next-hop retrieval target.

Input: fields `question` and `passages`. Output: one field `summary` meeting the requirements above.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

You are to produce exactly one compact second-hop retrieval query that targets the missing bridge or supporting evidence referenced by summary_1. Follow these operational rules when constructing the single `query` string:

- Use visible anchor tokens from `question` or `summary_1` (named works, titles, artist names, years or other identifiers only if present) and append a short retrieval cue naming the bridge sought (e.g., artist, album, recording, genre, season count, passage, supporting quote).
- Emit one concise line (one string assigned to `query`), roughly ≤ 10–12 words, formatted either as a short interrogative that names the anchors or as a compact keyword string (anchors + bridge keywords).
- If summary_1 explicitly requests a missing attribute or passage, emit the focused retrieval query rather than asking for clarification.
- Do not include answers, conclusions, enumerations, provenance notes, speculative language, or user-facing explanations; do not invent entities or add tokens that are not present in the inputs.
- Include year or other identifier tokens only if they appear in the provided inputs and help disambiguate.
- Keep wording retrieval-oriented and terse. Preserve the fallback that if retrieval using this query later fails, downstream steps may request clarification.

Output contract: produce exactly one field `query` whose value is the single compact retrieval query line described above.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single field only):
1. Merge the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into one compact integrated `summary` that preserves the primary answer name(s) and only includes passage-supported facts needed to justify or disambiguate that answer.
2. If `context` already contains a concise answer (a short sentence or a name), keep that content as the first sentence of `summary` when it is consistent with facts in `passages`. If `context` lacks a concise answer, create a concise first sentence that names the primary answer(s), using the canonical labels from `context` when available and otherwise from `passages`.
3. Expand the summary only when `passages` provide explicit, matching second-hop evidence about one or more named entities referenced in `context`. When expansion is appropriate, append at most one short supporting sentence per named entity. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's full name as shown in the inputs and paraphrase up to a few core attributes that are directly present in the `passages` (examples: nationality, a notable award/status, a current affiliation/team, a birth date, membership/partnership relation, or an explicit founding/formation fact).
4. Keep `summary` concise: typically 1–2 sentences for a single entity; allow up to 3 sentences only when multiple distinct named entities each require one supporting sentence. Prioritize the facts most directly necessary to answer the `question`.
5. Only assert facts that are directly supported by the provided `passages` or `context`. Do not introduce new entities, dates, awards, affiliations, roles, or causal claims that are not present in the inputs.
6. Prefer paraphrase over long verbatim copying. Use short quoted phrases only when the exact short phrase (roughly ≤10 words) appears in the `passages` and quoting preserves clarity.
7. If `context` and `passages` conflict, prefer facts that are explicitly present in the `passages`: update the concise answer to match passage-supported content rather than preserving a contradicted `context`.
8. If the retrieved `passages` do not supply explicit supporting evidence for named entities in `context`, keep the `summary` concise and do not expand or speculate.
9. Output exactly one field named `summary` whose value is the integrated post-retrieval summary text. Do not output additional fields, metadata, or commentary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Follow these operational rules when producing `answer`:
- Use only the content of `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2` as your evidential basis. Do not introduce new facts or external information.
- If both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, set `answer` to exactly one lowercase token: yes or no. The `answer` field must contain nothing else (no punctuation, capitalization, quotes, explanation, or additional text).
- Decide which token to emit by aggregating the visible summaries only. Treat `summary_2` as the later integrated evidence; do not let unresolved language in `summary_1` alone prevent emitting the single-token result if both summaries now unambiguously agree.
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, incomplete, hedged such that the relation is unresolved, or if the question is non-binary, do not apply the single-token rule; instead follow the agent's existing final-answer fallback behavior for unresolved or non-binary cases (which may produce a fuller response or an uncertainty signal).

Produce only the `answer` field as specified.

Matka Joanna | Matka Joanna is an album by Polish jazz trumpeter and composer Tomasz Stańko recorded in 1994 and released on the ECM label.  The music was inspired by Jerzy Kawalerowicz's 1961 movie "Matka Joanna od Aniołów".

Naya Zaher | Naya Zaher is a Hindi drama cum thriller movie of Bollywood directed by Jyoti Sarup and music direction made by Kalyanji–Anandji.  This movie was released in 1991 and it was India's first Hindi feature film based on HIV/AIDS.  The mode has given of an adult thriller, where the main villain in the film is not a person, but the disease, AIDS.

Lottery betting | Lottery betting is the activity of predicting the results of a lottery draw and placing a wager on the outcome.  Lottery betting (also known as lotto betting) is a form of online gambling, run by licensed betting firms, where players place bets on the outcome of lottery draws instead of buying physical or online  tickets via official lottery operators.

Matka Canyon | Matka (Macedonian: Матка , meaning "womb") is a canyon located west of central Skopje, Macedonia.  Covering roughly 5,000 hectares, Matka is one of the most popular outdoor destinations in Macedonia and is home to several medieval monasteries.  The Matka Lake within the Matka Canyon is the oldest artificial lake in the country.
response.summary_1:
- Fact (passage [1]): Matka gambling "originally involved betting on the opening and closing rates of cotton transmitted from the New York Cotton Exchange." (quoted fragment: "transmitted from the New York Cotton Exchange.")
- Fact (passage [2]): Dharmatma (1975 film) title character was based on matka king Ratan Khatri (paraphrase).
- Bridge entity: "matka" (Matka gambling) links the film's title character to the gambling practice (present and unambiguous in passage [2] and [1]).
- Missing/ambiguous facts: None for the question asked — the origin/source of the transmitted rates is explicitly given.
- Final answer (supported): The opening and closing cotton rates were transmitted from the New York Cotton Exchange (source: passage [1]).

hop2_docs: Matka gambling | Matka" gambling or satta" is a form of lottery which originally involved betting on the opening and closing rates of cotton transmitted from the New York Cotton Exchange.  It originates from before the era of Indian independence when it was known as "Ankada Jugar" ("figures gambling").  In the 1960s, the system was replaced with other ways of generating random numbers, including pulling slips from a large earthenware pot known as a "matka", or dealing playing cards.

Gambling in Omaha, Nebraska | Gambling in Omaha, Nebraska has been significant throughout the city's history.  From its founding in the 1850s through the 1930s, the city was known as a "wide-open" town, meaning that gambling of all sorts was accepted either openly or in closed quarters.  By the mid-20th century, Omaha reportedly had more illicit gambling per capita than any other city in the nation.  From the 1930s through the 1970s, the city's gambling was controlled by an Italian criminal element.

Cork, Blackrock and Passage Railway | The Cork, Blackrock and Passage Railway was a narrow gauge railway in County Cork, Ireland.  It was originally opened in 1850 as a railway between Cork and Passage West, but was converted to gauge in 1902.  An extension southwards from Passage West to Crosshaven opened in 1904.  The railway closed in 1932 and has since been replaced by a public pathway and nature area.

Extra-Long Staple Cotton Act of 1983 | The Extra-Long Staple Cotton Act of 1983 (P.L. 98-88) eliminated marketing quotas and allotments for extra-long staple cotton and tied its support to upland cotton through a formula that set the nonrecourse loan rate at not less than 150% of the upland cotton loan level.

Eremothecium gossypii | Eremothecium gossypii</small> (also known as Ashbya gossypii) is a filamentous fungus or mold closely related to yeast, but growing exclusively in a filamentous way.  It was originally isolated from cotton as a pathogen causing stigmatomycosis by Ashby and Nowell in 1926.  This disease affects the development of hair cells in cotton bolls and can be transmitted to citrus fruits, which thereupon dry out and collapse (dry rot disease).  In the first part of the 20th century, E. gossypii</small> and two other fungi causing stigmatomycosis (Eremothecium coryli</small> , Aureobasidium pullulans</small> ) made it virtually impossible to grow cotton in certain regions of the subtropics, causing severe economical losses.  Control of the spore-transmitting insects - cotton stainer (Dysdercus suturellus</small> ) and Antestiopsis</small> (antestia bugs) - permitted full eradication of infections.  E. gossypii</small> was recognized as a natural overproducer of riboflavin (vitamin B), which protects its spores against ultraviolet light.  This made it an interesting organism for industries, where genetically modified strains are still used to produce this vitamin.
Cotton School | Cotton School was a school of the Independent School District 2142 in Northern Minnesota.  It was located in Cotton, Minnesota.  The school opened in 1922, and closed in June 2011.

Matka Canyon | Matka (Macedonian: Матка , meaning "womb") is a canyon located west of central Skopje, Macedonia.  Covering roughly 5,000 hectares, Matka is one of the most popular outdoor destinations in Macedonia and is home to several medieval monasteries.  The Matka Lake within the Matka Canyon is the oldest artificial lake in the country.
response.summary_2: The opening and closing cotton rates were transmitted from the New York Cotton Exchange.
